Currently we have tracing proxy & setup code for Claude Code in this repo. I
  want to 1) enhance the setup code for better usability (currently many
  people struggles to activate tracing properly, and I think we need to just
  make the tracing available even when you launced claude with 'claude', not
  'claude-trace'.
  2) compile correct and complete guide of using this trace even when special
  requirement is added (e.g. "I haven't used Claude Code since setting it up
  because the Figma plugin couldn't be used together with the trace wrapper...
  However, plugins such as Figma don't seem to work properly within the
  VSCode extension. :blobcatmeltcry:
  Seungha Jeon  [1:37 PM]
  This issue occurs not only in the VSCode extension but also when using it
  directly from the terminal. Is anyone else running into the same problem?" -
  these kind of cases should be listed and tested)

  3) Support same kind of tracing for other coding agents as well. Scope:
  OpenCode, Codex, Cursor, Github Copilot
  And make the setup guide integrated, or provide separate guide based on
  implementation. There are many employees who works with different agents in
  switching manner, and I want tracing setup should not be too difficult for
  them as well.
  (Traces from different agents should be saved in different directory, e.g.
  opencode-traces, cursor-traces... and so on)

  If you think other features should be added as well, please let me know.

  Let's analyze the requirements thoroughly and implement correctly, test it
  and give me the full setup guide to test out.

The following deferred tools are now available via ToolSearch. Their schemas are NOT loaded — calling them directly will fail with InputValidationError. Use ToolSearch with query "select:<name>[,<name>...]" to load tool schemas before calling them:

ExitWorktree

Monitor
NotebookEdit
PushNotification

TaskGet
TaskList
TaskOutput

TaskUpdate
WebFetch
WebSearch

Available agent types for the Agent tool:
- claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)
- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)
- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)
- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

When you launch multiple agents for independent work, send them in a single message with multiple tool uses so they run concurrently.

The following skills are available for use with the Skill tool:

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	#
3	# setup.sh — one-time setup for Claude Code trace capture (with reasoning).
4	#
5	# What this does:
6	#   1. Installs a small local pass-through proxy and its Python deps.
7	#   2. Adds a `claude-trace` shell function that starts the proxy (if needed)
8	#      and runs Claude Code pointed at it.
9	#
10	# After running this once, just use `claude-trace` instead of `claude`.
11	# Everything else about Claude Code stays the same.
12	#
13	set -euo pipefail
14	
15	INSTALL_DIR="${CC_TRACE_HOME:-$HOME/.cc-trace}"
16	TRACE_DIR="${CC_TRACE_DIR:-$HOME/claude-traces}"
17	PORT="${CC_TRACE_PORT:-8788}"
18	
19	echo "==> Installing Claude Code trace capture"
20	
21	mkdir -p "$INSTALL_DIR" "$TRACE_DIR"
22	
23	# --- 1. drop the proxy file next to this script ---
24	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
25	if [ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/cc_trace_proxy.py" ]; then
26	  cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/cc_trace_proxy.py" "$INSTALL_DIR/cc_trace_proxy.py"
27	else
28	  echo "ERROR: cc_trace_proxy.py not found next to setup.sh" >&2
29	  exit 1
30	fi
31	
32	# --- 2. python deps in a dedicated venv (avoids PEP 668 / Homebrew issues) ---
33	echo "==> Creating virtual environment and installing deps (httpx, starlette, uvicorn)"
34	VENV="$INSTALL_DIR/venv"
35	if [ ! -x "$VENV/bin/python" ]; then
36	  python3 -m venv "$VENV"
37	fi
38	"$VENV/bin/python" -m pip install --quiet httpx starlette uvicorn
39	PYTHON_BIN="$VENV/bin/python"
40	
41	# --- 3. write a launcher the user calls instead of `claude` ---
42	cat > "$INSTALL_DIR/claude-trace" << EOF
43	#!/usr/bin/env bash
44	# Launcher: ensure the trace proxy is up, apply this launch's provider config,
45	# then run Claude Code through it.
46	#
47	# Provider knobs are plain env vars, applied PER LAUNCH (no pkill needed):
48	#   CC_TRACE_UPSTREAM            upstream API (default https://api.anthropic.com)
49	#   CC_STRIP_THINKING_DISPLAY=1  strip thinking.display (non-Anthropic providers)
50	#   CC_TRACE_INJECT_FIELDS       JSON merged into forwarded model calls
51	# Unset vars reset to defaults. Note: config is global to the proxy — the most
52	# recent launch's settings apply to all sessions.
53	set -euo pipefail
54	PORT=${PORT}
55	TRACE_DIR="${TRACE_DIR}"
56	PROXY="${INSTALL_DIR}/cc_trace_proxy.py"
57	PYTHON="${PYTHON_BIN}"
58	
59	# start proxy if it isn't already listening (local health endpoint; not traced)
60	if ! curl -s -o /dev/null --max-time 1 "http://127.0.0.1:\${PORT}/cc-trace/health" 2>/dev/null; then
61	  CC_TRACE_DIR="\$TRACE_DIR" CC_TRACE_PORT="\$PORT" \\
62	    nohup "\$PYTHON" "\$PROXY" > "${INSTALL_DIR}/proxy.log" 2>&1 &
63	  # wait briefly for it to come up
64	  for i in \$(seq 1 20); do
65	    curl -s -o /dev/null --max-time 1 "http://127.0.0.1:\${PORT}/cc-trace/health" 2>/dev/null && break
66	    sleep 0.2
67	  done
68	fi
69	
70	# apply this launch's config to the (possibly already-running) proxy
71	INJ="\${CC_TRACE_INJECT_FIELDS:-}"; [ -z "\$INJ" ] && INJ='{}'
72	STRIP=false; [ "\${CC_STRIP_THINKING_DISPLAY:-}" = "1" ] && STRIP=true
73	curl -s -o /dev/null -X POST -H 'content-type: application/json' \\
74	  -d "{\"upstream\":\"\${CC_TRACE_UPSTREAM:-https://api.anthropic.com}\",\"strip_thinking_display\":\$STRIP,\"inject_fields\":\$INJ}" \\
75	  "http://127.0.0.1:\${PORT}/cc-trace/config" || true
76	
77	# point Claude Code at the proxy and run it (passing through any args)
78	export ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL="http://127.0.0.1:\${PORT}"
79	export ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=true   # keep MCP tool search working behind a custom base URL
80	exec claude "\$@"
81	EOF
82	chmod +x "$INSTALL_DIR/claude-trace"
83	
84	# --- 4. enable summarized thinking in Claude Code's user settings ---
85	# Needed so the API returns summarized reasoning text (otherwise Opus 4.8
86	# omits it). We merge the key in without disturbing existing settings, and
87	# back up the file first.
88	echo "==> Enabling showThinkingSummaries in ~/.claude/settings.json"
89	SETTINGS="$HOME/.claude/settings.json"
90	mkdir -p "$HOME/.claude"
91	"$PYTHON_BIN" - "$SETTINGS" << 'PYEOF'
92	import json, os, sys, shutil
93	
94	path = sys.argv[1]
95	data = {}
96	if os.path.exists(path):
97	    try:
98	        with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
99	            data = json.load(f)
100	        if not isinstance(data, dict):
101	            print(f"   (existing settings.json is not a JSON object; leaving it alone)")
102	            sys.exit(0)
103	    except json.JSONDecodeError:
104	        print(f"   (existing settings.json isn't valid JSON; leaving it alone — "
105	              f"please add '\"showThinkingSummaries\": true' manually)")
106	        sys.exit(0)
107	    # back up before modifying
108	    shutil.copy2(path, path + ".pre-cc-trace.bak")
109	
110	if data.get("showThinkingSummaries") is True:
111	    print("   (already enabled)")
112	    sys.exit(0)
113	
114	data["showThinkingSummaries"] = True
115	with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
116	    json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
117	    f.write("\n")
118	print("   (done)")
119	PYEOF
120	
121	# --- 5. add to PATH via shell rc ---
122	RC=""
123	case "${SHELL##*/}" in
124	  zsh)  RC="$HOME/.zshrc" ;;
125	  bash) RC="$HOME/.bashrc" ;;
126	  *)    RC="$HOME/.profile" ;;
127	esac
128	
129	LINE="export PATH=\"$INSTALL_DIR:\$PATH\"   # cc-trace"
130	if ! grep -qF "# cc-trace" "$RC" 2>/dev/null; then
131	  echo "$LINE" >> "$RC"
132	  echo "==> Added $INSTALL_DIR to PATH in $RC"
133	fi
134	
135	echo ""
136	echo "==> Done."
137	echo "    Open a new terminal (or run: source $RC)"
138	echo "    Then use 'claude-trace' instead of 'claude'."
139	echo "    Traces are written to: $TRACE_DIR"

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""
3	cc_trace_proxy — a transparent pass-through proxy for Claude Code.
4	
5	Claude Code is pointed at this proxy via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL. The proxy relays
6	every request to the upstream API and streams the response back chunk-by-chunk
7	as it arrives (no buffering, so time-to-first-token is unaffected). On stream
8	completion it writes the full request and response — including extended-thinking
9	/ reasoning content — to JSON files on disk.
10	
11	Auth headers are forwarded as-is and the bytes the client sees are identical to
12	what the upstream returned.
13	"""
14	
15	import json
16	import os
17	import sys
18	import time
19	import uuid
20	from datetime import datetime, timezone
21	
22	try:
23	    import httpx
24	    from starlette.applications import Starlette
25	    from starlette.requests import Request
26	    from starlette.responses import JSONResponse, Response, StreamingResponse
27	    from starlette.routing import Route
28	    import uvicorn
29	except ImportError:
30	    sys.stderr.write(
31	        "Missing dependencies. Install with:\n"
32	        "  pip install --user httpx starlette uvicorn\n"
33	    )
34	    sys.exit(1)
35	
36	# Runtime configuration. Env vars give the initial values; the launcher can
37	# update them at any time via POST /cc-trace/config (no proxy restart needed),
38	# so each `claude-trace` launch applies its own provider settings.
39	#   upstream                — API to forward to (Anthropic, Friendli, ...)
40	#   strip_thinking_display  — remove thinking.display (providers that reject it)
41	#   inject_fields           — JSON object merged into forwarded model calls
42	def _parse_inject(raw: str) -> dict:
43	    try:
44	        v = json.loads(raw or "{}")
45	        return v if isinstance(v, dict) else {}
46	    except json.JSONDecodeError:
47	        sys.stderr.write("[cc-trace] inject_fields is not valid JSON; ignoring\n")
48	        return {}
49	
50	
51	CONFIG = {
52	    "upstream": os.environ.get("CC_TRACE_UPSTREAM", "https://api.anthropic.com").rstrip("/"),
53	    "strip_thinking_display": os.environ.get("CC_STRIP_THINKING_DISPLAY", "") == "1",
54	    "inject_fields": _parse_inject(os.environ.get("CC_TRACE_INJECT_FIELDS", "")),
55	}
56	
57	TRACE_DIR = os.environ.get("CC_TRACE_DIR", os.path.expanduser("~/claude-traces"))
58	PORT = int(os.environ.get("CC_TRACE_PORT", "8788"))
59	
60	# Headers we must NOT forward upstream (hop-by-hop / host-specific).
61	SKIP_REQUEST_HEADERS = {"host", "content-length", "accept-encoding", "connection"}
62	SKIP_RESPONSE_HEADERS = {"content-length", "content-encoding", "transfer-encoding", "connection"}
63	
64	os.makedirs(TRACE_DIR, exist_ok=True)
65	
66	# One shared client: connection pooling + keep-alive across all requests.
67	CLIENT = httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=None)
68	
69	
70	def _ts() -> str:
71	    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%dT%H%M%S_%f")
72	
73	
74	def _write(path: str, data) -> None:
75	    try:
76	        with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
77	            if isinstance(data, (bytes, bytearray)):
78	                f.write(data.decode("utf-8", errors="replace"))
79	            else:
80	                f.write(data)
81	    except Exception as e:  # never let logging break the proxy
82	        sys.stderr.write(f"[cc-trace] failed to write {path}: {e}\n")
83	
84	
85	def _parse_sse_to_message(raw_text: str):
86	    """
87	    Reconstruct the final Messages API response object from a streamed SSE body.
88	    This rebuilds content blocks (including `thinking` blocks) so the saved
89	    response mirrors a non-streaming response body, with reasoning intact.
90	    Best-effort: on any problem we fall back to saving the raw stream.
91	    """
92	    message = None
93	    blocks = {}  # index -> block dict
94	    for line in raw_text.splitlines():
95	        line = line.strip()
96	        if not line.startswith("data:"):
97	            continue
98	        payload = line[len("data:"):].strip()
99	        if not payload or payload == "[DONE]":
100	            continue
101	        try:
102	            evt = json.loads(payload)
103	        except json.JSONDecodeError:
104	            continue
105	        etype = evt.get("type")
106	        if etype == "message_start":
107	            message = evt.get("message", {})
108	            message["content"] = []
109	        elif etype == "content_block_start":
110	            idx = evt.get("index")
111	            blocks[idx] = dict(evt.get("content_block", {}))
112	            for k in ("text", "thinking", "partial_json"):
113	                if k in blocks[idx] and blocks[idx][k] is None:
114	                    blocks[idx][k] = ""
115	        elif etype == "content_block_delta":
116	            idx = evt.get("index")
117	            delta = evt.get("delta", {})
118	            b = blocks.setdefault(idx, {})
119	            dt = delta.get("type")
120	            if dt == "text_delta":
121	                b["text"] = b.get("text", "") + delta.get("text", "")
122	            elif dt == "thinking_delta":
123	                b["thinking"] = b.get("thinking", "") + delta.get("thinking", "")
124	            elif dt == "signature_delta":
125	                b["signature"] = b.get("signature", "") + delta.get("signature", "")
126	            elif dt == "input_json_delta":
127	                b["partial_json"] = b.get("partial_json", "") + delta.get("partial_json", "")
128	        elif etype == "message_delta":
129	            if message is not None:
130	                d = evt.get("delta", {})
131	                message.update({k: v for k, v in d.items()})
132	                if "usage" in evt:
133	                    message.setdefault("usage", {}).update(evt["usage"])
134	    if message is None:
135	        return None
136	    # finalize tool_use inputs
137	    for b in blocks.values():
138	        if b.get("type") == "tool_use" and "partial_json" in b:
139	            try:
140	                b["input"] = json.loads(b.pop("partial_json") or "{}")
141	            except json.JSONDecodeError:
142	                pass
143	    message["content"] = [blocks[i] for i in sorted(blocks.keys())]
144	    return message
145	
146	
147	def _strip_display(req_json):
148	    """Remove thinking.display from the request, if present. Returns (body, changed)."""
149	    if not isinstance(req_json, dict):
150	        return req_json, False
151	    thinking = req_json.get("thinking")
152	    if not isinstance(thinking, dict) or "display" not in thinking:
153	        return req_json, False
154	    new = dict(req_json)
155	    new_thinking = dict(thinking)
156	    new_thinking.pop("display", None)
157	    new["thinking"] = new_thinking
158	    return new, True
159	
160	
161	def _save_response(rid: str, raw: bytes, ctype: str) -> None:
162	    """Write the captured response to disk (reconstructing SSE if streamed)."""
163	    if "text/event-stream" in ctype:
164	        text = raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
165	        rebuilt = _parse_sse_to_message(text)
166	        if rebuilt is not None:
167	            _write(os.path.join(TRACE_DIR, f"{rid}.response.json"),
168	                   json.dumps(rebuilt, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
169	        # always keep the raw stream too, as ground truth
170	        _write(os.path.join(TRACE_DIR, f"{rid}.response.sse"), text)
171	        return
172	    try:
173	        _write(os.path.join(TRACE_DIR, f"{rid}.response.json"),
174	               json.dumps(json.loads(raw), ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
175	    except json.JSONDecodeError:
176	        _write(os.path.join(TRACE_DIR, f"{rid}.response.raw"), raw)
177	
178	
179	async def health(request: Request) -> Response:
180	    """Local health endpoint: answered by the proxy itself, never forwarded
181	    or traced. Reports the live config so `curl` shows current provider."""
182	    return JSONResponse({"status": "ok", **CONFIG})
183	
184	
185	async def set_config(request: Request) -> Response:
186	    """Update runtime config (upstream / strip / inject) without a restart.
187	    The launcher calls this on every claude-trace invocation. Omitted keys
188	    are left unchanged; the launcher always sends all three."""
189	    try:
190	        data = json.loads(await request.body())
191	        assert isinstance(data, dict)
192	    except Exception:
193	        return JSONResponse({"error": "body must be a JSON object"}, status_code=400)
194	    if "upstream" in data:
195	        CONFIG["upstream"] = str(data["upstream"]).rstrip("/")
196	    if "strip_thinking_display" in data:
197	        CONFIG["strip_thinking_display"] = data["strip_thinking_display"] in (True, 1, "1", "true")
198	    if "inject_fields" in data:
199	        CONFIG["inject_fields"] = data["inject_fields"] if isinstance(data["inject_fields"], dict) else {}
200	    sys.stderr.write(f"[cc-trace] config updated: {CONFIG}\n")
201	    return JSONResponse(CONFIG)
202	
203	
204	async def handler(request: Request) -> Response:
205	    body = await request.body()
206	    path = request.url.path
207	    query = ("?" + request.url.query) if request.url.query else ""
208	
209	    # Only trace requests that carry a body (model calls etc.); plain GETs
210	    # (health probes, model lists) are forwarded but not written to disk.
211	    trace = bool(body)
212	    rid = f"{_ts()}_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}" if trace else None
213	
214	    # --- save request (full, unmodified) ---
215	    req_json = None
216	    if body:
217	        try:
218	            req_json = json.loads(body)
219	        except json.JSONDecodeError:
220	            pass
221	    if trace:
222	        if req_json is not None:
223	            _write(os.path.join(TRACE_DIR, f"{rid}.request.json"),
224	                   json.dumps(req_json, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
225	        else:
226	            _write(os.path.join(TRACE_DIR, f"{rid}.request.raw"), body)
227	
228	    # --- optionally rewrite the FORWARDED body (live config) ---
229	    upstream_base = CONFIG["upstream"]
230	    inject = CONFIG["inject_fields"]
231	    fwd_body = body
232	    if isinstance(req_json, dict):
233	        new_json, changed = req_json, False
234	        if CONFIG["strip_thinking_display"]:
235	            new_json, changed = _strip_display(new_json)
236	        # inject provider-specific fields only into model-call bodies
237	        if inject and "model" in new_json and "messages" in new_json:
238	            merged = dict(new_json)
239	            merged.update(inject)
240	            new_json, changed = merged, True
241	        if changed:
242	            fwd_body = json.dumps(new_json).encode("utf-8")
243	
244	    fwd_headers = {k: v for k, v in request.headers.items()
245	                   if k.lower() not in SKIP_REQUEST_HEADERS}
246	
247	    started = time.time()
248	    upstream_req = CLIENT.build_request(
249	        request.method, f"{upstream_base}{path}{query}",
250	        headers=fwd_headers, content=fwd_body,
251	    )
252	    try:
253	        upstream = await CLIENT.send(upstream_req, stream=True)
254	    except httpx.HTTPError as e:
255	        sys.stderr.write(f"[cc-trace] upstream error for {path}: {e}\n")
256	        return JSONResponse({"error": f"cc-trace proxy: upstream unreachable ({e})"},
257	                            status_code=502)
258	
259	    ctype = upstream.headers.get("content-type", "")
260	    out_headers = {k: v for k, v in upstream.headers.items()
261	                   if k.lower() not in SKIP_RESPONSE_HEADERS}
262	
263	    async def relay():
264	        """Yield chunks to the client as they arrive; save the trace at the end."""
265	        chunks = []
266	        try:
267	            async for chunk in upstream.aiter_bytes():
268	                chunks.append(chunk)
269	                yield chunk
270	        finally:
271	            await upstream.aclose()
272	            if trace:
273	                _save_response(rid, b"".join(chunks), ctype)
274	            sys.stderr.write(
275	                f"[cc-trace] {request.method} {path} -> {upstream.status_code} "
276	                f"({(time.time()-started)*1000:.0f}ms)"
277	                + (f" saved {rid}\n" if trace else "\n")
278	            )
279	
280	    return StreamingResponse(relay(), status_code=upstream.status_code,
281	                             headers=out_headers)
282	
283	
284	def build_app():
285	    return Starlette(routes=[
286	        Route("/cc-trace/health", health, methods=["GET"]),
287	        Route("/cc-trace/config", set_config, methods=["POST"]),
288	        Route("/{path:path}", handler,
289	              methods=["GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE", "PATCH", "OPTIONS"]),
290	    ])
291	
292	
293	if __name__ == "__main__":
294	    app = build_app()
295	    sys.stderr.write(
296	        f"[cc-trace] proxy on http://127.0.0.1:{PORT}\n"
297	        f"[cc-trace] writing traces to {TRACE_DIR}\n"
298	        f"[cc-trace] config: {CONFIG}\n"
299	    )
300	    uvicorn.run(app, host="127.0.0.1", port=PORT, log_level="warning")

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""
3	cc_trace_proxy_enhanced — enhanced pass-through proxy for Claude Code.
4	
5	Builds on cc_trace_proxy with:
6	  - Health check endpoint        (GET /health)
7	  - Stats endpoint               (GET /stats — token usage, latency, model breakdown)
8	  - Trace search endpoint        (GET /traces?q=keyword&model=...&limit=N)
9	  - Request replay               (POST /replay/{trace_id} — re-send a captured request)
10	  - API key redaction             (strip sensitive keys from saved traces)
11	  - Trace rotation               (auto-delete traces older than N days / beyond N files)
12	  - Rate limiting                (token-bucket per-IP, configurable)
13	  - CORS support                  (for browser-based dashboards)
14	  - Web dashboard                (GET / — minimal HTML trace browser)
15	  - Error webhook                (POST to external URL on upstream errors)
16	"""
17	
18	import asyncio
19	import gzip
20	import json
21	import math
22	import os
23	import re
24	import sys
25	import time
26	import uuid
27	from collections import defaultdict
28	from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
29	from pathlib import Path
30	
31	try:
32	    import httpx
33	    from starlette.applications import Starlette
34	    from starlette.requests import Request
35	    from starlette.responses import HTMLResponse, JSONResponse, Response, StreamingResponse
36	    from starlette.routing import Route
37	    import uvicorn
38	except ImportError:
39	    sys.stderr.write(
40	        "Missing dependencies. Install with:\n"
41	        "  pip install --user httpx starlette uvicorn\n"
42	    )
43	    sys.exit(1)
44	
45	# ── Configuration ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
46	
47	UPSTREAM = os.environ.get("CC_TRACE_UPSTREAM", "https://api.anthropic.com").rstrip("/")
48	TRACE_DIR = os.environ.get("CC_TRACE_DIR", os.path.expanduser("~/claude-traces"))
49	PORT = int(os.environ.get("CC_TRACE_PORT", "8788"))
50	
51	STRIP_THINKING_DISPLAY = os.environ.get("CC_STRIP_THINKING_DISPLAY", "") == "1"
52	FORCE_SUMMARIZED = os.environ.get("CC_FORCE_SUMMARIZED", "") == "1"
53	
54	try:
55	    INJECT_FIELDS = json.loads(os.environ.get("CC_TRACE_INJECT_FIELDS", "") or "{}")
56	    if not isinstance(INJECT_FIELDS, dict):
57	        INJECT_FIELDS = {}
58	except json.JSONDecodeError:
59	    sys.stderr.write("[cc-trace] CC_TRACE_INJECT_FIELDS is not valid JSON; ignoring\n")
60	    INJECT_FIELDS = {}
61	
62	# Enhanced features — env vars
63	REDACT_KEYS = os.environ.get("CC_TRACE_REDACT", "1") == "1"
64	TRACE_MAX_AGE_DAYS = int(os.environ.get("CC_TRACE_MAX_AGE_DAYS", "0"))  # 0 = disabled
65	TRACE_MAX_FILES = int(os.environ.get("CC_TRACE_MAX_FILES", "0"))  # 0 = disabled
66	RATE_LIMIT_RPS = float(os.environ.get("CC_TRACE_RATE_LIMIT", "0"))  # 0 = disabled
67	WEBHOOK_URL = os.environ.get("CC_TRACE_WEBHOOK_URL", "")
68	CORS_ORIGIN = os.environ.get("CC_TRACE_CORS_ORIGIN", "*")
69	COMPRESS_TRACES = os.environ.get("CC_TRACE_COMPRESS", "") == "1"  # gzip .json files
70	
71	SKIP_REQUEST_HEADERS = {"host", "content-length", "accept-encoding", "connection"}
72	SKIP_RESPONSE_HEADERS = {"content-length", "content-encoding", "transfer-encoding", "connection"}
73	
74	os.makedirs(TRACE_DIR, exist_ok=True)
75	
76	# ── In-memory stats ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
77	
78	class Stats:
79	    def __init__(self):
80	        self.start_time = time.time()
81	        self.total_requests = 0
82	        self.total_errors = 0
83	        self.total_input_tokens = 0
84	        self.total_output_tokens = 0
85	        self.latencies: list[float] = []  # last 1000
86	        self.by_model: dict[str, dict] = defaultdict(lambda: {"count": 0, "input": 0, "output": 0, "errors": 0})
87	        self.by_status: dict[int, int] = defaultdict(int)
88	        self._lock = asyncio.Lock()
89	
90	    async def record(self, model: str | None, status: int, latency_s: float,
91	                      input_tokens: int = 0, output_tokens: int = 0, is_error: bool = False):
92	        async with self._lock:
93	            self.total_requests += 1
94	            if is_error:
95	                self.total_errors += 1
96	            self.total_input_tokens += input_tokens
97	            self.total_output_tokens += output_tokens
98	            self.latencies.append(latency_s)
99	            if len(self.latencies) > 1000:
100	                self.latencies = self.latencies[-1000:]
101	            if model:
102	                e = self.by_model[model]
103	                e["count"] += 1
104	                e["input"] += input_tokens
105	                e["output"] += output_tokens
106	                if is_error:
107	                    e["errors"] += 1
108	            self.by_status[status] += 1
109	
110	    def snapshot(self) -> dict:
111	        lats = self.latencies
112	        p50 = p95 = p99 = avg = 0.0
113	        if lats:
114	            s = sorted(lats)
115	            n = len(s)
116	            p50 = s[int(n * 0.5)]
117	            p95 = s[int(n * 0.95)]
118	            p99 = s[int(n * 0.99)]
119	            avg = sum(s) / n
120	        uptime = time.time() - self.start_time
121	        return {
122	            "uptime_s": round(uptime, 1),
123	            "total_requests": self.total_requests,
124	            "total_errors": self.total_errors,
125	            "total_input_tokens": self.total_input_tokens,
126	            "total_output_tokens": self.total_output_tokens,
127	            "latency_ms": {
128	                "avg": round(avg * 1000, 1),
129	                "p50": round(p50 * 1000, 1),
130	                "p95": round(p95 * 1000, 1),
131	                "p99": round(p99 * 1000, 1),
132	            },
133	            "by_model": dict(self.by_model),
134	            "by_status": dict(self.by_status),
135	        }
136	
137	stats = Stats()
138	
139	# ── Rate limiter (token-bucket per IP) ──────────────────────────────────────────
140	
141	class RateLimiter:
142	    def __init__(self, rps: float):
143	        self.rps = rps
144	        self.buckets: dict[str, tuple[float, float]] = {}  # ip -> (tokens, last_refill)
145	        self._lock = asyncio.Lock()
146	
147	    async def allow(self, ip: str) -> bool:
148	        if self.rps <= 0:
149	            return True
150	        async with self._lock:
151	            now = time.time()
152	            tokens, last = self.buckets.get(ip, (self.rps * 2, now))
153	            elapsed = now - last
154	            tokens = min(tokens + elapsed * self.rps, self.rps * 2)
155	            if tokens >= 1.0:
156	                self.buckets[ip] = (tokens - 1.0, now)
157	                return True
158	            self.buckets[ip] = (tokens, now)
159	            return False
160	
161	rate_limiter = RateLimiter(RATE_LIMIT_RPS)
162	
163	# ── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
164	
165	def _ts() -> str:
166	    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%dT%H%M%S_%f")
167	
168	
169	def _write(path: str, data, compress: bool = False) -> None:
170	    try:
171	        if compress:
172	            with gzip.open(path + ".gz", "wt", encoding="utf-8") as f:
173	                f.write(data if isinstance(data, str) else data.decode("utf-8", errors="replace"))
174	        else:
175	            with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
176	                if isinstance(data, (bytes, bytearray)):
177	                    f.write(data.decode("utf-8", errors="replace"))
178	                else:
179	                    f.write(data)
180	    except Exception as e:
181	        sys.stderr.write(f"[cc-trace] failed to write {path}: {e}\n")
182	
183	
184	_REDACT_KEY_RE = re.compile(
185	    r"(anthropic[-_]?auth[-_]?token|x-api-key|authorization|api[-_]?key)",
186	    re.IGNORECASE,
187	)
188	_REDACT_VALUE_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^(sk-|key-|Bearer\s+)", re.IGNORECASE)
189	
190	
191	def _redact(obj):
192	    """Recursively redact API-key-like values in dicts/lists."""
193	    if isinstance(obj, dict):
194	        out = {}
195	        for k, v in obj.items():
196	            if isinstance(v, str) and _REDACT_KEY_RE.search(k):
197	                out[k] = "[REDACTED]"
198	            elif isinstance(v, str) and _REDACT_VALUE_PATTERN.match(v):
199	                out[k] = "[REDACTED]"
200	            else:
201	                out[k] = _redact(v)
202	        return out
203	    if isinstance(obj, list):
204	        return [_redact(i) for i in obj]
205	    return obj
206	
207	
208	def _extract_token_counts(msg: dict | None) -> tuple[int, int]:
209	    """Return (input_tokens, output_tokens) from a reconstructed message."""
210	    if not isinstance(msg, dict):
211	        return 0, 0
212	    usage = msg.get("usage", {})
213	    return usage.get("input_tokens", 0), usage.get("output_tokens", 0)
214	
215	
216	def _send_webhook(payload: dict) -> None:
217	    """Fire-and-forget webhook notification."""
218	    if not WEBHOOK_URL:
219	        return
220	    try:
221	        import threading
222	        def _post():
223	            try:
224	                with httpx.Client(timeout=5.0) as c:
225	                    c.post(WEBHOOK_URL, json=payload)
226	            except Exception:
227	                pass
228	        threading.Thread(target=_post, daemon=True).start()
229	    except Exception:
230	        pass
231	
232	
233	def _rotate_traces() -> None:
234	    """Remove old traces if rotation is configured."""
235	    if TRACE_MAX_AGE_DAYS <= 0 and TRACE_MAX_FILES <= 0:
236	        return
237	    try:
238	        trace_path = Path(TRACE_DIR)
239	        files = sorted(trace_path.iterdir(), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime)
240	        # Age-based rotation
241	        if TRACE_MAX_AGE_DAYS > 0:
242	            cutoff = time.time() - TRACE_MAX_AGE_DAYS * 86400
243	            for f in files:
244	                if f.stat().st_mtime < cutoff:
245	                    f.unlink(missing_ok=True)
246	            files = [f for f in files if f.exists()]
247	        # Count-based rotation
248	        if TRACE_MAX_FILES > 0:
249	            # Group by request ID (stem before .request / .response)
250	            rids: dict[str, float] = {}
251	            for f in files:
252	                stem = f.name.split(".")[0]
253	                rids.setdefault(stem, f.stat().st_mtime)
254	            if len(rids) > TRACE_MAX_FILES:
255	                sorted_rids = sorted(rids.items(), key=lambda x: x[1])
256	                to_remove = sorted_rids[: len(rids) - TRACE_MAX_FILES]
257	                for rid, _ in to_remove:
258	                    for f in trace_path.iterdir():
259	                        if f.name.startswith(rid + "."):
260	                            f.unlink(missing_ok=True)
261	    except Exception as e:
262	        sys.stderr.write(f"[cc-trace] rotation error: {e}\n")
263	
264	
265	# ── SSE parser (same logic as original, kept here for self-containment) ─────────
266	
267	def _parse_sse_to_message(raw_text: str):
268	    message = None
269	    blocks = {}
270	    for line in raw_text.splitlines():
271	        line = line.strip()
272	        if not line.startswith("data:"):
273	            continue
274	        payload = line[len("data:"):].strip()
275	        if not payload or payload == "[DONE]":
276	            continue
277	        try:
278	            evt = json.loads(payload)
279	        except json.JSONDecodeError:
280	            continue
281	        etype = evt.get("type")
282	        if etype == "message_start":
283	            message = evt.get("message", {})
284	            message["content"] = []
285	        elif etype == "content_block_start":
286	            idx = evt.get("index")
287	            blocks[idx] = dict(evt.get("content_block", {}))
288	            for k in ("text", "thinking", "partial_json"):
289	                if k in blocks[idx] and blocks[idx][k] is None:
290	                    blocks[idx][k] = ""
291	        elif etype == "content_block_delta":
292	            idx = evt.get("index")
293	            delta = evt.get("delta", {})
294	            b = blocks.setdefault(idx, {})
295	            dt = delta.get("type")
296	            if dt == "text_delta":
297	                b["text"] = b.get("text", "") + delta.get("text", "")
298	            elif dt == "thinking_delta":
299	                b["thinking"] = b.get("thinking", "") + delta.get("thinking", "")
300	            elif dt == "signature_delta":
301	                b["signature"] = b.get("signature", "") + delta.get("signature", "")
302	            elif dt == "input_json_delta":
303	                b["partial_json"] = b.get("partial_json", "") + delta.get("partial_json", "")
304	        elif etype == "message_delta":
305	            if message is not None:
306	                d = evt.get("delta", {})
307	                message.update({k: v for k, v in d.items()})
308	                if "usage" in evt:
309	                    message.setdefault("usage", {}).update(evt["usage"])
310	    if message is None:
311	        return None
312	    for b in blocks.values():
313	        if b.get("type") == "tool_use" and "partial_json" in b:
314	            try:
315	                b["input"] = json.loads(b.pop("partial_json") or "{}")
316	            except json.JSONDecodeError:
317	                pass
318	    message["content"] = [blocks[i] for i in sorted(blocks.keys())]
319	    return message
320	
321	
322	def _inject_summarized(req_json):
323	    if not isinstance(req_json, dict):
324	        return req_json, False
325	    thinking = req_json.get("thinking")
326	    if not isinstance(thinking, dict):
327	        return req_json, False
328	    if thinking.get("type") == "disabled":
329	        return req_json, False
330	    if thinking.get("display") == "summarized":
331	        return req_json, False
332	    new = dict(req_json)
333	    new_thinking = dict(thinking)
334	    new_thinking["display"] = "summarized"
335	    new["thinking"] = new_thinking
336	    return new, True
337	
338	
339	def _strip_display(req_json):
340	    if not isinstance(req_json, dict):
341	        return req_json, False
342	    thinking = req_json.get("thinking")
343	    if not isinstance(thinking, dict) or "display" not in thinking:
344	        return req_json, False
345	    new = dict(req_json)
346	    new_thinking = dict(thinking)
347	    new_thinking.pop("display", None)
348	    new["thinking"] = new_thinking
349	    return new, True
350	
351	
352	# ── CORS middleware helper ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
353	
354	def _cors_response(response: Response) -> Response:
355	    response.headers["Access-Control-Allow-Origin"] = CORS_ORIGIN
356	    response.headers["Access-Control-Allow-Methods"] = "GET, POST, OPTIONS"
357	    response.headers["Access-Control-Allow-Headers"] = "Content-Type, Authorization"
358	    return response
359	
360	
361	# ── Request handlers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
362	
363	async def health(request: Request) -> Response:
364	    return _cors_response(JSONResponse({"status": "ok", "upstream": UPSTREAM}))
365	
366	
367	async def get_stats(request: Request) -> Response:
368	    return _cors_response(JSONResponse(stats.snapshot()))
369	
370	
371	async def search_traces(request: Request) -> Response:
372	    """Search saved traces by keyword, model, or status code."""
373	    q = request.query_params.get("q", "").lower()
374	    model = request.query_params.get("model", "")
375	    limit = min(int(request.query_params.get("limit", "50")), 200)
376	    trace_path = Path(TRACE_DIR)
377	
378	    results = []
379	    request_files = sorted(trace_path.glob("*.request.json*"), reverse=True)
380	    if COMPRESS_TRACES:
381	        request_files = sorted(trace_path.glob("*.request.json.gz"), reverse=True)
382	
383	    for rf in request_files:
384	        if len(results) >= limit:
385	            break
386	        rid = rf.name.split(".")[0]
387	        try:
388	            if rf.suffix == ".gz":
389	                with gzip.open(rf, "rt", encoding="utf-8") as f:
390	                    req = json.load(f)
391	            else:
392	                with open(rf, encoding="utf-8") as f:
393	                    req = json.load(f)
394	        except Exception:
395	            continue
396	
397	        req_model = req.get("model", "")
398	        if model and model.lower() != req_model.lower():
399	            continue
400	        if q and not _keyword_match(req, q):
401	            continue
402	
403	        # Find corresponding response
404	        resp_file = trace_path / f"{rid}.response.json"
405	        resp_size = resp_file.stat().st_size if resp_file.exists() else None
406	        status_code = None
407	        if resp_file.exists():
408	            try:
409	                with open(resp_file, encoding="utf-8") as f:
410	                    resp = json.load(f)
411	                status_code = resp.get("_status_code")
412	                # check stop_reason as a signal
413	            except Exception:
414	                pass
415	
416	        results.append({
417	            "id": rid,
418	            "model": req_model,
419	            "timestamp": rid.split("_")[0] if "_" in rid else rid,
420	            "response_size": resp_size,
421	            "status_code": status_code,
422	        })
423	
424	    return _cors_response(JSONResponse({"traces": results, "count": len(results)}))
425	
426	
427	async def replay_trace(request: Request) -> Response:
428	    """Re-send a previously captured request to the upstream API."""
429	    trace_id = request.path_params["trace_id"]
430	    trace_path = Path(TRACE_DIR)
431	
432	    req_file = trace_path / f"{trace_id}.request.json"
433	    if not req_file.exists():
434	        return _cors_response(JSONResponse({"error": "trace not found"}, status_code=404))
435	
436	    try:
437	        with open(req_file, encoding="utf-8") as f:
438	            req_body = json.load(f)
439	    except Exception as e:
440	        return _cors_response(JSONResponse({"error": f"failed to read trace: {e}"}, status_code=500))
441	
442	    # Determine the original request path — default to /v1/messages
443	    api_path = req_body.pop("_original_path", "/v1/messages")
444	    fwd_headers = dict(request.headers)
445	    fwd_headers.pop("host", None)
446	    fwd_headers.pop("content-length", None)
447	
448	    started = time.time()
449	    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=None) as client:
450	        upstream = await client.request(
451	            "POST",
452	            f"{UPSTREAM}{api_path}",
453	            headers=fwd_headers,
454	            content=json.dumps(req_body).encode("utf-8"),
455	        )
456	
457	    latency_ms = (time.time() - started) * 1000
458	    raw = upstream.content
459	    ctype = upstream.headers.get("content-type", "")
460	
461	    # Save replayed response as a new trace
462	    replay_id = f"{_ts()}_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}_replay"
463	    _write(os.path.join(TRACE_DIR, f"{replay_id}.request.json"),
464	           json.dumps(req_body, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), compress=COMPRESS_TRACES)
465	
466	    if "text/event-stream" in ctype:
467	        text = raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
468	        rebuilt = _parse_sse_to_message(text)
469	        if rebuilt is not None:
470	            _write(os.path.join(TRACE_DIR, f"{replay_id}.response.json"),
471	                   json.dumps(rebuilt, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), compress=COMPRESS_TRACES)
472	        _write(os.path.join(TRACE_DIR, f"{replay_id}.response.sse"), text)
473	    else:
474	        try:
475	            _write(os.path.join(TRACE_DIR, f"{replay_id}.response.json"),
476	                   json.dumps(json.loads(raw), ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), compress=COMPRESS_TRACES)
477	        except json.JSONDecodeError:
478	            _write(os.path.join(TRACE_DIR, f"{replay_id}.response.raw"), raw)
479	
480	    return _cors_response(JSONResponse({
481	        "replay_id": replay_id,
482	        "status_code": upstream.status_code,
483	        "latency_ms": round(latency_ms, 1),
484	    }))
485	
486	
487	_DASHBOARD_HTML = """<!DOCTYPE html>
488	<html lang="en"><head><meta charset="utf-8"><meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1">
489	<title>cc-trace dashboard</title>
490	<style>
491	*{box-sizing:border-box;margin:0;padding:0}
492	body{font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Roboto,sans-serif;background:#0f172a;color:#e2e8f0;padding:2rem}
493	h1{font-size:1.5rem;margin-bottom:1rem;color:#38bdf8}
494	h2{font-size:1.1rem;margin:1.2rem 0 .6rem;color:#94a3b8}
495	.stats{display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(160px,1fr));gap:.8rem;margin-bottom:1.5rem}
496	.stat{background:#1e293b;border-radius:8px;padding:1rem;text-align:center}
497	.stat .num{font-size:1.6rem;font-weight:700;color:#38bdf8}
498	.stat .lbl{font-size:.75rem;color:#64748b;margin-top:.2rem}
499	table{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;background:#1e293b;border-radius:8px;overflow:hidden}
500	th,td{text-align:left;padding:.6rem .8rem;border-bottom:1px solid #334155;font-size:.85rem}
501	th{color:#94a3b8;font-weight:600;background:#0f172a}
502	td{color:#cbd5e1}
503	a{color:#38bdf8;text-decoration:none}
504	a:hover{text-decoration:underline}
505	.search{display:flex;gap:.6rem;margin-bottom:1rem}
506	.search input{flex:1;padding:.5rem .8rem;background:#1e293b;border:1px solid #334155;border-radius:6px;color:#e2e8f0;font-size:.9rem}
507	.search button{padding:.5rem 1.2rem;background:#38bdf8;color:#0f172a;border:none;border-radius:6px;font-weight:600;cursor:pointer}
508	.search button:hover{background:#7dd3fc}
509	.badge{display:inline-block;padding:.15rem .5rem;border-radius:4px;font-size:.75rem;font-weight:600}
510	.badge-ok{background:#065f46;color:#6ee7b7}
511	.badge-err{background:#7f1d1d;color:#fca5a5}
512	.refresh{float:right;font-size:.8rem;color:#64748b;cursor:pointer}
513	.refresh:hover{color:#94a3b8}
514	</style></head><body>
515	<h1>cc-trace dashboard</h1>
516	<span class="refresh" onclick="loadStats();loadTraces();">↻ refresh</span>
517	<div class="stats" id="stats"></div>
518	<div class="search">
519	  <input id="q" placeholder="Search traces..." onkeydown="if(event.key==='Enter')loadTraces()">
520	  <input id="model" placeholder="Filter model...">
521	  <button onclick="loadTraces()">Search</button>
522	</div>
523	<h2>Recent traces</h2>
524	<table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Model</th><th>Time</th><th>Size</th><th>Status</th><th>Replay</th></tr></thead>
525	<tbody id="traces"></tbody></table>
526	<script>
527	async function loadStats(){
528	  const r=await fetch('/stats');const d=await r.json();const el=document.getElementById('stats');
529	  const items=[
530	    ['Requests',d.total_requests],['Errors',d.total_errors],
531	    ['Input tokens',d.total_input_tokens.toLocaleString()],
532	    ['Output tokens',d.total_output_tokens.toLocaleString()],
533	    ['Avg latency',d.latency_ms.avg+'ms'],['p95',d.latency_ms.p95+'ms'],['p99',d.latency_ms.p99+'ms'],
534	    ['Uptime',Math.floor(d.uptime_s/60)+'m'],
535	  ];
536	  el.innerHTML=items.map(([l,v])=>'<div class="stat"><div class="num">'+v+'</div><div class="lbl">'+l+'</div></div>').join('');
537	}
538	async function loadTraces(){
539	  const q=document.getElementById('q').value;const m=document.getElementById('model').value;
540	  const r=await fetch('/traces?q='+encodeURIComponent(q)+'&model='+encodeURIComponent(m)+'&limit=80');
541	  const d=await r.json();const tb=document.getElementById('traces');
542	  tb.innerHTML=d.traces.map(t=>{
543	    const sc=t.status_code||200;const cls=sc>=400?'badge-err':'badge-ok';
544	    return '<tr><td><a href="/trace/'+t.id+'">'+t.id+'</a></td><td>'+t.model+'</td><td>'+t.timestamp+
545	      '</td><td>'+(t.response_size?Math.round(t.response_size/1024)+'K':'-')+
546	      '</td><td><span class="badge '+cls+'">'+sc+'</span></td>'+
547	      '<td><button onclick="replay(\\''+t.id+'\\')">Replay</button></td></tr>';
548	  }).join('');
549	}
550	async function replay(id){if(!confirm('Replay request '+id+'?'))return;
551	  const r=await fetch('/replay/'+id,{method:'POST'});const d=await r.json();
552	  alert('Replay '+d.replay_id+': status '+d.status_code+', '+d.latency_ms+'ms');
553	}
554	loadStats();loadTraces();
555	</script></body></html>
556	"""
557	
558	
559	async def dashboard(request: Request) -> Response:
560	    return _cors_response(HTMLResponse(_DASHBOARD_HTML))
561	
562	
563	async def trace_detail(request: Request) -> Response:
564	    """Return the stored request + response for a trace ID."""
565	    trace_id = request.path_params["trace_id"]
566	    trace_path = Path(TRACE_DIR)
567	    result: dict = {"id": trace_id}
568	
569	    # Request
570	    req_file = trace_path / f"{trace_id}.request.json"
571	    req_gz = trace_path / f"{trace_id}.request.json.gz"
572	    if req_file.exists():
573	        try:
574	            with open(req_file, encoding="utf-8") as f:
575	                result["request"] = json.load(f)
576	        except Exception:
577	            result["request"] = None
578	    elif req_gz.exists():
579	        try:
580	            with gzip.open(req_gz, "rt", encoding="utf-8") as f:
581	                result["request"] = json.load(f)
582	        except Exception:
583	            result["request"] = None
584	    else:
585	        return _cors_response(JSONResponse({"error": "trace not found"}, status_code=404))
586	
587	    # Response
588	    resp_file = trace_path / f"{trace_id}.response.json"
589	    resp_gz = trace_path / f"{trace_id}.response.json.gz"
590	    if resp_file.exists():
591	        try:
592	            with open(resp_file, encoding="utf-8") as f:
593	                result["response"] = json.load(f)
594	        except Exception:
595	            result["response"] = None
596	    elif resp_gz.exists():
597	        try:
598	            with gzip.open(resp_gz, "rt", encoding="utf-8") as f:
599	                result["response"] = json.load(f)
600	        except Exception:
601	            result["response"] = None
602	    else:
603	        result["response"] = None
604	
605	    return _cors_response(JSONResponse(result))
606	
607	
608	def _keyword_match(obj, q: str) -> bool:
609	    """Recursively check if any string value in obj contains q."""
610	    if isinstance(obj, str):
611	        return q in obj.lower()
612	    if isinstance(obj, dict):
613	        return any(_keyword_match(v, q) for v in obj.values())
614	    if isinstance(obj, list):
615	        return any(_keyword_match(v, q) for v in obj)
616	    return False
617	
618	
619	# ── Main proxy handler ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
620	
621	async def handler(request: Request) -> Response:
622	    # Rate limit check
623	    client_ip = request.client.host if request.client else "unknown"
624	    if not await rate_limiter.allow(client_ip):
625	        return Response(content=b'{"error":"rate limited"}', status_code=429,
626	                        media_type="application/json")
627	
628	    body = await request.body()
629	    rid = f"{_ts()}_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
630	    path = request.url.path
631	    query = ("?" + request.url.query) if request.url.query else ""
632	
633	    # --- save request (redacted if configured) ---
634	    req_json = None
635	    try:
636	        req_json = json.loads(body) if body else {}
637	        to_save = _redact(req_json) if REDACT_KEYS else req_json
638	        # stash the original path for replay support
639	        if isinstance(to_save, dict):
640	            to_save = dict(to_save)
641	            to_save["_original_path"] = path
642	        _write(os.path.join(TRACE_DIR, f"{rid}.request.json"),
643	               json.dumps(to_save, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), compress=COMPRESS_TRACES)
644	    except json.JSONDecodeError:
645	        _write(os.path.join(TRACE_DIR, f"{rid}.request.raw"), body)
646	
647	    # --- optionally rewrite the FORWARDED body ---
648	    fwd_body = body
649	    if req_json is not None and isinstance(req_json, dict):
650	        new_json, changed = req_json, False
651	        if STRIP_THINKING_DISPLAY:
652	            new_json, changed = _strip_display(new_json)
653	        elif FORCE_SUMMARIZED:
654	            new_json, changed = _inject_summarized(new_json)
655	        if INJECT_FIELDS and "model" in new_json and "messages" in new_json:
656	            merged = dict(new_json)
657	            merged.update(INJECT_FIELDS)
658	            new_json, changed = merged, True
659	        if changed:
660	            fwd_body = json.dumps(new_json).encode("utf-8")
661	
662	    fwd_headers = {k: v for k, v in request.headers.items()
663	                   if k.lower() not in SKIP_REQUEST_HEADERS}
664	
665	    started = time.time()
666	    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=None) as client:
667	        upstream = await client.request(
668	            request.method,
669	            f"{UPSTREAM}{path}{query}",
670	            headers=fwd_headers,
671	            content=fwd_body,
672	        )
673	
674	    raw = upstream.content
675	    ctype = upstream.headers.get("content-type", "")
676	    status = upstream.status_code
677	    latency_s = time.time() - started
678	    is_error = status >= 400
679	
680	    # --- save response ---
681	    rebuilt = None
682	    saved = False
683	    if "text/event-stream" in ctype:
684	        text = raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
685	        rebuilt = _parse_sse_to_message(text)
686	        if rebuilt is not None:
687	            to_save_r = _redact(rebuilt) if REDACT_KEYS else rebuilt
688	            _write(os.path.join(TRACE_DIR, f"{rid}.response.json"),
689	                   json.dumps(to_save_r, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), compress=COMPRESS_TRACES)
690	            saved = True
691	        _write(os.path.join(TRACE_DIR, f"{rid}.response.sse"), text)
692	        saved = True
693	    if not saved:
694	        try:
695	            resp_json = json.loads(raw)
696	            to_save_r = _redact(resp_json) if REDACT_KEYS else resp_json
697	            _write(os.path.join(TRACE_DIR, f"{rid}.response.json"),
698	                   json.dumps(to_save_r, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), compress=COMPRESS_TRACES)
699	        except json.JSONDecodeError:
700	            _write(os.path.join(TRACE_DIR, f"{rid}.response.raw"), raw)
701	
702	    # --- update stats ---
703	    model = req_json.get("model") if isinstance(req_json, dict) else None
704	    input_t, output_t = _extract_token_counts(rebuilt) if rebuilt else (0, 0)
705	    if not input_t and not output_t and not is_error:
706	        # try to get from non-streamed response
707	        try:
708	            resp_json = json.loads(raw)
709	            input_t, output_t = _extract_token_counts(resp_json)
710	        except Exception:
711	            pass
712	    await stats.record(model, status, latency_s, input_t, output_t, is_error)
713	
714	    # --- webhook on error ---
715	    if is_error:
716	        _send_webhook({
717	            "event": "upstream_error",
718	            "trace_id": rid,
719	            "status_code": status,
720	            "model": model,
721	            "latency_ms": round(latency_s * 1000, 1),
722	            "path": path,
723	        })
724	
725	    # --- trace rotation ---
726	    _rotate_traces()
727	
728	    sys.stderr.write(
729	        f"[cc-trace] {request.method} {path} -> {status} "
730	        f"({latency_s*1000:.0f}ms) saved {rid}"
731	        f" tokens={input_t}+{output_t}\n"
732	    )
733	
734	    # --- return upstream response byte-for-byte ---
735	    out_headers = {k: v for k, v in upstream.headers.items()
736	                   if k.lower() not in SKIP_RESPONSE_HEADERS}
737	    return Response(content=raw, status_code=status,
738	                    headers=out_headers, media_type=ctype or None)
739	
740	
741	# ── App wiring ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
742	
743	def build_app():
744	    return Starlette(routes=[
745	        Route("/", dashboard, methods=["GET"]),
746	        Route("/health", health, methods=["GET"]),
747	        Route("/stats", get_stats, methods=["GET"]),
748	        Route("/traces", search_traces, methods=["GET", "OPTIONS"]),
749	        Route("/trace/{trace_id}", trace_detail, methods=["GET"]),
750	        Route("/replay/{trace_id}", replay_trace, methods=["POST", "OPTIONS"]),
751	        Route("/{path:path}", handler,
752	              methods=["GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE", "PATCH", "OPTIONS"]),
753	    ])
754	
755	
756	if __name__ == "__main__":
757	    app = build_app()
758	    sys.stderr.write(
759	        f"[cc-trace-enhanced] proxy on http://127.0.0.1:{PORT}  ->  {UPSTREAM}\n"
760	        f"[cc-trace-enhanced] writing traces to {TRACE_DIR}\n"
761	        f"[cc-trace-enhanced] features: redact={REDACT_KEYS} rotate_age={TRACE_MAX_AGE_DAYS}d "
762	        f"rotate_max={TRACE_MAX_FILES} rate_limit={RATE_LIMIT_RPS}rps "
763	        f"webhook={'on' if WEBHOOK_URL else 'off'} compress={COMPRESS_TRACES}\n"
764	    )
765	    uvicorn.run(app, host="127.0.0.1", port=PORT, log_level="warning")
766

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# claude_friendli_setup.sh
3	# Configures ~/.claude/settings.json to use FriendliAI's model API with Claude Code.
4	# Fetches the live model list from FriendliAI at run time so the config stays
5	# up-to-date each time you re-run the script.
6	#
7	# Usage:
8	#   chmod +x claude_friendli_setup.sh
9	#   ./claude_friendli_setup.sh            # set up FriendliAI
10	#   ./claude_friendli_setup.sh --restore  # restore original Anthropic settings
11	
12	set -euo pipefail
13	
14	FRIENDLI_API="https://api.friendli.ai/serverless"
15	SETTINGS_DIR="${HOME}/.claude"
16	SETTINGS_FILE="${SETTINGS_DIR}/settings.json"
17	BACKUP_FILE="${SETTINGS_DIR}/settings.json.pre-friendli.bak"
18	
19	# ── Restore mode ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
20	if [[ "${1:-}" == "--restore" ]]; then
21	  echo ""
22	  echo "→ Restoring original Anthropic settings..."
23	  if [[ -f "$BACKUP_FILE" ]]; then
24	    cp "$BACKUP_FILE" "$SETTINGS_FILE"
25	    echo "  ✓ Restored from backup: ${BACKUP_FILE}"
26	  else
27	    echo "  No backup found. Removing FriendliAI keys from current settings..."
28	    python3 -c "
29	import json, sys
30	with open(sys.argv[1]) as f:
31	    s = json.load(f)
32	env = s.get('env', {})
33	for k in ['ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN','ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL','API_TIMEOUT_MS','CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_GATEWAY_MODEL_DISCOVERY']:
34	    env.pop(k, None)
35	s.pop('model', None)
36	s.pop('availableModels', None)
37	if env:
38	    s['env'] = env
39	elif 'env' in s:
40	    del s['env']
41	print(json.dumps(s, indent=2))
42	" "$SETTINGS_FILE" > /tmp/restored_settings.json && mv /tmp/restored_settings.json "$SETTINGS_FILE"
43	    echo "  ✓ FriendliAI keys removed."
44	  fi
45	  echo ""
46	  echo "  Anthropic models are now accessible again."
47	  echo "  Run 'claude' to start."
48	  echo ""
49	  exit 0
50	fi
51	
52	# ── 1. Prompt for API key ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
53	echo ""
54	echo "┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐"
55	echo "│          FriendliAI × Claude Code Setup                     │"
56	echo "└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘"
57	echo ""
58	echo "  You'll need a FriendliAI Personal API Key to continue."
59	echo "  Find or create yours here:"
60	echo ""
61	echo "  https://friendli.ai/docs/guides/suite/personal-api-keys#personal-api-keys"
62	echo ""
63	read -rp "  Enter your FriendliAI API key: " FRIENDLI_API_KEY
64	echo ""
65	
66	if [[ -z "$FRIENDLI_API_KEY" ]]; then
67	  echo "Error: No API key entered. Aborting."
68	  exit 1
69	fi
70	
71	# ── 2. Fetch available models from FriendliAI ─────────────────────────────────
72	echo "→ Verifying key and fetching model list from FriendliAI..."
73	
74	HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /tmp/friendli_models.json -w "%{http_code}" \
75	  -H "Authorization: Bearer ${FRIENDLI_API_KEY}" \
76	  "${FRIENDLI_API}/v1/models")
77	
78	if [[ "$HTTP_STATUS" != "200" ]]; then
79	  echo ""
80	  echo "Error: FriendliAI API returned HTTP ${HTTP_STATUS}."
81	  echo "       Please check your API key and try again."
82	  exit 1
83	fi
84	
85	MODELS_JSON=$(cat /tmp/friendli_models.json)
86	rm -f /tmp/friendli_models.json
87	
88	if command -v python3 &>/dev/null; then
89	  FRIENDLI_MODEL_IDS=$(python3 -c "
90	import sys, json
91	data = json.loads(sys.argv[1])
92	ids = [m['id'] for m in data.get('data', []) if 'id' in m]
93	print(json.dumps(ids))
94	" "$MODELS_JSON")
95	elif command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
96	  FRIENDLI_MODEL_IDS=$(echo "$MODELS_JSON" | jq '[.data[].id]')
97	else
98	  echo "Error: Neither python3 nor jq is available. Install one to proceed."
99	  exit 1
100	fi
101	
102	FRIENDLI_COUNT=$(python3 -c "import sys,json; print(len(json.loads(sys.argv[1])))" "$FRIENDLI_MODEL_IDS")
103	echo "   ✓ Key valid. Found ${FRIENDLI_COUNT} FriendliAI model(s)."
104	
105	# ── 3. Read existing settings and extract current Anthropic models ────────────
106	mkdir -p "$SETTINGS_DIR"
107	
108	EXISTING="{}"
109	EXISTING_ANTHROPIC_MODELS="[]"
110	
111	if [[ -f "$SETTINGS_FILE" ]]; then
112	  EXISTING=$(cat "$SETTINGS_FILE")
113	  # Extract any pre-existing availableModels that are NOT FriendliAI models
114	  EXISTING_ANTHROPIC_MODELS=$(python3 -c "
115	import sys, json
116	s = json.loads(sys.argv[1])
117	friendli_ids = set(json.loads(sys.argv[2]))
118	existing = s.get('availableModels', [])
119	# Keep only models that aren't already in the FriendliAI list
120	anthropic = [m for m in existing if m not in friendli_ids]
121	print(json.dumps(anthropic))
122	" "$EXISTING" "$FRIENDLI_MODEL_IDS")
123	fi
124	
125	ANTHROPIC_COUNT=$(python3 -c "import sys,json; print(len(json.loads(sys.argv[1])))" "$EXISTING_ANTHROPIC_MODELS")
126	if (( ANTHROPIC_COUNT > 0 )); then
127	  echo "   ✓ Preserving ${ANTHROPIC_COUNT} existing Anthropic model(s) in /model picker."
128	  echo "     Note: Anthropic models will appear in the picker but require"

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Cursor's "Override OpenAI Base URL" feature and whether requests route through Cursor's servers:

## How the Base URL Override Works

The "Override OpenAI Base URL" feature is found in Cursor's settings. You launch Cursor and open Settings, click on Models in the left sidebar, and at the top of the Models section you'll see an "Override OpenAI Base URL" field where you enter your custom endpoint.

When configured, the override directly redirects Cursor's API calls. Cursor is designed to work with OpenAI's API format. Since OpenAI-compatible APIs implement the same format, you can trick Cursor into thinking it's talking to OpenAI when it's actually talking to your custom model. By overriding the base URL, you redirect all of Cursor's API calls from OpenAI's servers to your custom endpoint.

Regarding the URL format itself: You enter your endpoint URL, and you must make sure to include the /v1 path suffix — Cursor appends /chat/completions to whatever you provide.

## Does It Route Through Cursor's Servers?

This is the key nuance, and the answer is **it depends on the feature you're using**:

**Plan/Chat mode** uses your custom endpoint directly. Cursor only honors the custom OpenAI base URL for plan mode — the chat/planning panel (Cmd/Ctrl + L). Everything else still uses Cursor's own backend, even after you save the custom key.

**Agent/Composer and other features do NOT use your custom endpoint.** Cursor's coding agent (Composer, inline edit, autocomplete, apply/edit) does not work with external OpenAI-compatible endpoints — those features are locked to Cursor's own backend and will not route through the custom endpoint. Only the chat/plan panel honors the custom API key + base URL.

**Tab completions always use Cursor's models.** Tab completions are not affected by BYOK settings; they always use Cursor's built-in models.

## Important Caveat for Inspecting/Proxying Requests

If your goal is to proxy or inspect the full prompts (a common reason people want this), note that there are known complications. Some providers require special handling of Cursor's request format. For example, OpenRouter created a dedicated endpoint specifically because of this: OpenRouter exposes a dedicated Cursor endpoint at /api/v1/cursor that normalizes Cursor's request format into the standard OpenAI Chat Completions format before routing.

## Summary

When you use the "Override OpenAI Base URL," requests for the **chat/plan panel** go directly to your specified endpoint (not through Cursor's servers for the inference itself). However, **Cursor's agentic features (Composer, inline edits, apply, autocomplete, Tab)** remain locked to Cursor's own backend regardless of the override setting. So it's not a global redirect of all traffic — it only affects a subset of Cursor's functionality.

If you need a full coding agent that routes entirely through a custom endpoint, tools like Claude Code, Codex CLI, Cline, Continue CLI, or Hermes Agent are recommended instead.

Links: [{"title":"Advanced Configuration – Codex | OpenAI Developers","url":"https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-advanced"},{"title":"Allow overriding the base URL for OpenAI in config.toml · Issue #11698 · openai/codex","url":"https://github.com/openai/codex/issues/11698"},{"title":"Codex Desktop mixes local custom provider base_url with remote auth.json API key · Issue #24457 · openai/codex","url":"https://github.com/openai/codex/issues/24457"},{"title":"Local model base_url not working · Issue #7152 · openai/codex","url":"https://github.com/openai/codex/issues/7152"},{"title":"Codex with Azure OpenAI in Microsoft Foundry Models - Microsoft Foundry | Microsoft Learn","url":"https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/openai/how-to/codex"},{"title":"Codex CLI Multi-Provider Setup via config.toml","url":"https://ofox.ai/blog/codex-cli-custom-model-providers-byo-setup/"}]

Here's what I found about configuring a custom base URL for the OpenAI Codex CLI:

## Quick Option: `openai_base_url` (simplest)

If you just want to point the built-in OpenAI provider at a proxy or router, you don't need to define a whole new provider. If you just need to point the built-in OpenAI provider at an LLM proxy, router, or data-residency enabled project, set openai_base_url in config.toml instead of defining a new provider. This changes the base URL for the built-in openai provider without requiring a separate model_providers.<id> entry.

This is important because of a known limitation: if you set `model_provider = "openai"` with `[model_providers.openai]` and a custom `base_url`, Codex doesn't seem to respect it — since it's a built-in provider, overrides in the TOML aren't taken into account. So for the built-in OpenAI provider, use the dedicated `openai_base_url` key rather than trying to redefine the provider.

## Defining a Custom Model Provider

For non-OpenAI or self-hosted endpoints, define a custom provider. A model provider defines how Codex connects to a model (base URL, wire API, authentication, and optional HTTP headers). Custom providers can't reuse the reserved built-in provider IDs: openai, ollama, and lmstudio.

The config lives in your config file. Codex CLI reads ~/.codex/config.toml on every invocation, and the [model_providers.<id>] table is where it expects custom endpoints to live.

A typical custom provider block looks like this:

```toml
model = "your-model-name"
model_provider = "myprovider"

[model_providers.myprovider]
name = "My Provider"
base_url = "https://api.example.com/v1"
env_key = "MY_API_KEY"
wire_api = "chat"   # or "responses"
```

Key notes on the fields:
- `base_url` points at the API root, ending in `/v1` for OpenAI-compatible gateways, with no trailing slash.
- `env_key` names the environment variable that holds the API key (e.g., export `MY_API_KEY="..."`).
- `wire_api` is either `chat` or `responses` depending on the endpoint's API style.

## Azure OpenAI Example

For Azure, the official configuration uses a custom provider with the `responses` wire API: model = "gpt-5-codex", model_provider = "azure", with [model_providers.azure] specifying name = "Azure OpenAI", base_url = "https://YOUR_RESOURCE_NAME.openai.azure.com/openai/v1", env_key = "AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY", and wire_api = "responses". Then you export the API key environment variable that the config file references, e.g. `export AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY="<your-api-key>"`.

## Profiles for Switching Configs

You can save multiple named configurations and switch between them: Profiles let you save named configuration layers and switch between them from the CLI. When you pass --profile profile-name, Codex loads ~/.codex/config.toml, then overlays ~/.codex/profile-name.config.toml.

## Summary

- **For the built-in OpenAI provider through a proxy:** use the `openai_base_url` key (don't try to override `[model_providers.openai]`, which is ignored).
- **For custom/third-party/self-hosted endpoints:** define a `[model_providers.<id>]` block with `base_url`, `env_key`, and `wire_api`, then set `model_provider` to that ID.
- **Environment variable alternative:** `OPENAI_BASE_URL` + `OPENAI_API_KEY` in your shell works for a single endpoint, while config.toml is better for managing multiple providers.

You can find the full details in the [OpenAI Codex Advanced Configuration docs](https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-advanced).

Here's what I found regarding GitHub Copilot, custom proxies, request interception, and enterprise/HTTP_PROXY configuration:

## Official GitHub Copilot Proxy Behavior (Enterprise)

GitHub's official documentation explains how Copilot works with corporate proxies. By default, Copilot connects to GitHub's server directly from your environment via a secure HTTPS connection, and you don't necessarily need to configure any additional network settings to use Copilot.

However, in enterprise/corporate environments: Some networks use an HTTP proxy server to intercept Internet traffic before sending it to its intended location. Companies often use an HTTP proxy to detect suspicious traffic or restrict the content entering their networks. If you're working on a corporate network, you may need to configure Copilot to connect via an HTTP proxy.

### Environment Variables (HTTP_PROXY)
Regarding the `HTTP_PROXY` variable specifically: If you don't configure a proxy directly in your editor, Copilot checks if a proxy URL is set in any of several environment variables, listed from highest to lowest priority. You can use any of these variables to store the URL of a standard HTTP proxy.

An important caveat about how Copilot handles these variables differently from convention: In standard usage, the http and https portions of these variables refer to the type of request being made, not the URL of the proxy itself. Copilot does not follow this convention and uses the URL stored in the variable with the highest priority as the proxy for both HTTP and HTTPS requests.

### Proxy Limitations & Enterprise Requirements
- Copilot supports basic HTTP proxy setups. If you need to authenticate to a proxy, Copilot supports basic authentication or authentication with Kerberos. If the proxy URL starts https://, the proxy is not currently supported.
- GitHub Copilot uses custom code to connect to proxies. This means a proxy setup supported by your editor is not necessarily supported by GitHub Copilot.
- For enterprises: If you are an employee of a company with a proxy server, your company must also configure proxy settings for Copilot at the company level.

### Certificate Interception Errors
A common enterprise issue when intercepting requests: Depending on your proxy setup, you may encounter errors like "certificate signature failure," "custom certificate," or "unable to verify the first certificate." These errors are usually caused by a corporate proxy setup that uses custom certificates to intercept and inspect secure connections. Suggested fixes include configuring a proxy that doesn't intercept secure connections, or ensuring the custom certificates are properly installed in your operating system's trust store.

## Community/Third-Party Proxies (Intercepting api.githubcopilot.com)

Several open-source projects intercept and forward Copilot requests, though these are **unofficial and reverse-engineered**:

- **ClaraLeigh/proxy-for-copilot** — This custom proxy forwards HTTP requests to their original destination, except when talking to the Copilot chat endpoints. When it finds that endpoint it modifies the request to use GPT-4 using the main openai endpoint. It works by configuring your application to use the proxy server by setting the HTTP_PROXY and HTTPS_PROXY environment variables to http://localhost:8090, then the proxy will intercept and modify the specified requests.

- **ericc-ch/copilot-api** — This is a reverse-engineered proxy of GitHub Copilot API. It is not supported by GitHub, and may break unexpectedly. Use at your own risk.

- **voidsteed/copilot-proxy-api** — Supports business and enterprise accounts, e.g., you can use it with an enterprise plan GitHub account via `npx copilot-proxy-api@latest start --account-type enterprise`.

⚠️ **Important warning** for these third-party tools: Excessive automated or scripted use of Copilot (including rapid or bulk requests, such as via automated tools) may trigger GitHub's abuse-detection systems. You may receive a warning from GitHub Security, and further anomalous activity could result in temporary suspension of your Copilot access. GitHub prohibits use of their servers for excessive automated bulk activity or any activity that places undue burden on their infrastructure.

If you're configuring this for a legitimate enterprise proxy setup, I'd recommend relying on the official GitHub documentation approach rather than the reverse-engineered community proxies. Let me know if you'd like more detail on any specific aspect.
